message:
fix(codegen): Handle PyValue-returning functions in constructor calls

Fixes compilation errors when magic methods returning PyValue call class
constructors that return error unions.

Problem:
- Magic methods like __eq__, __gt__ return PyValue (not !PyValue)
- Inside these methods, calling Class.init() which returns !Class
- Generated code used `try Class.init()` which requires error union return type
- This caused: "error: function cannot return an error"

Solution:
- Check current_function_returns_pyvalue flag in constructor call codegen
- When true, use `catch unreachable` instead of `try`
- Generated code: `(Class.init() catch unreachable)` instead of `(try Class.init())`
- Panics on init failure, matching Python's exception propagation behavior

Also added missing cases for new PyValue variants:
- int_convert.zig: Added .object and .not_implemented cases to error message switch

Changes:
- src/codegen/native/expressions/calls.zig:1289-1292, 1336-1339: Check flag before emitting `try`
- src/codegen/native/expressions/calls.zig:1553-1556: Use `catch unreachable` when in PyValue-returning function
- packages/runtime/src/runtime/int_convert.zig:37-38: Handle new PyValue variants

Testing:
- test_bool now compiles successfully (was failing with 2 compilation errors)
- test_eq.py continues to work with vtable implementation
